article_title: Does minimally invasive percutaneous transilial internal fixator became an effective option for sacral fractures? A prospective study with novel implantation technique
journal: European Journal of Trauma and Emergency Surgery
label: Fig. 1
caption: A Prone position on radiolucent table, marking of PSIS and greater trochanter on each side. B PSIS exposure: starting point located caudal and medial to PSIS (Anatomic entry point). C Rongeur bite used on the medial aspect of PSIS to reach the cancellous bone

article_title: Does minimally invasive percutaneous transilial internal fixator became an effective option for sacral fractures? A prospective study with novel implantation technique
journal: European Journal of Trauma and Emergency Surgery
label: Fig. 2
caption: A Awl used for penetration and creating screw tunnel. B Alternatively, oscillating drill could be used instead. C, D direction of awl in ventral and caudal direction. E Pedicular screw insertion directed caudal, ventral towards greater trochanter aiming towards AIIS, then connection bar inserted submuscular and fixed to the pedicle screws

label: Fig. 3
caption: A Showing Obturator view with awl insertion between the 2 tables. B Screw placement above sciatic notch in iliac view. C Tear drop view, D Iliac outlet view. E Obturator inlet. F True lateral view

article_title: Modified spinopelvic crab-shaped fixation using offset connectors for a H-shaped sacral fracture with a floating Roy-Camille type 3 transverse component: a case report
label: Fig. 1
caption: Computed tomography (CT) on admission (A) Three-dimensional CT reconstruction of the pelvis demonstrating bilateral sacral fractures, bilateral ischiopubic fractures, and right femoral intertrochanteric/subtrochanteric fractures. B, C Three-dimensional CT reconstruction of the sacrum (anteroposterior view in B and posteroanterior view in C) demonstrating an H-shaped sacral fracture (right: approximately 8-mm vertical displacement in Denis zone I-II; left: Denis zone I-II; transverse component at the S1-2 level). D, E Sagittal (D) and axial (E) CT images demonstrating a Roy-Camille type 3 transverse component and a large free fragment occupying the S1 spinal canal

article_title: Modified spinopelvic crab-shaped fixation using offset connectors for a H-shaped sacral fracture with a floating Roy-Camille type 3 transverse component: a case report
label: Fig. 2
caption: Schematic depiction of sacral fracture lines (red) The distal sacral fragment (asterisk), classified as Roy-Camille type 3, was a floating fragment. The right side involved Denis zone I-II, and the left side involved Denis zone II

article_title: Modified spinopelvic crab-shaped fixation using offset connectors for a H-shaped sacral fracture with a floating Roy-Camille type 3 transverse component: a case report
label: Fig. 3
caption: Preoperative and intraoperative photographs (A) Preoperative photograph showing bilateral 5-cm incisions above the posterior superior iliac spine (PSIS) for placement of L5 pedicle screws and iliac screws, and an 8-cm midline incision for laminectomy and reduction. B Intraoperative ultrasonography after laminectomy showing a large free fragment (asterisk) within the spinal canal. C The free fragment (arrowhead) is exposed after dural retraction with a nerve dissector (dura intact). D Magnified view of panel C

article_title: Modified spinopelvic crab-shaped fixation using offset connectors for a H-shaped sacral fracture with a floating Roy-Camille type 3 transverse component: a case report
label: Fig. 4
caption: Reduction maneuver and buttress technique (A) Bilateral neurodissectors were inserted lateral to the dura into the transverse fracture site, with the tips positioned on the ventral cortex of the proximal sacrum. B A Kapandji-like levering maneuver is used to reduce the floating fragment. C Offset connectors positioned along the dorsal cortex as a buttress to prevent dorsal redisplacement, ensuring no protrusion beyond the spinous process tips. D The S2 spinous process (arrow) was clamped and fixed with a transverse connector (arrowhead)
